extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a can only be true when the full 6-way conjunction holds (g6pd_deficiency AND qt_prolongation AND anticoagulated AND hypertension AND hyperkalemia AND renal_impairment), via rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50); otherwise it defaults to false. To violate the property both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b must be true, which requires the full conjunction. In exactly that region, two rules match rec_drug_b: guard_suppress_b (priority 90) sets it false, and rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) sets it true (g6pd_deficiency is part of the conjunction). Per-output highest priority wins, so guard_suppress_b (90 > 50) dominates and forces rec_drug_b := false. Thus whenever rec_drug_a is true, rec_drug_b is forced false; when the conjunction is false, rec_drug_a is false and the property holds trivially. No input can make both jointly true. The property holds.
